title: Believers
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Redlink|hide}}
{{Youmay|the [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]]|the [[Wizards.com]] article ''[[The Believers]]''}}

|formed=

{{Quote|The "Believers" are a group of individuals devoted to the study and practice of the dark side of the Force. They are misguided.|Jedi Master Kirlocca|<ref name="Further Discussions With Lanius">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20020829lanius|text=Further Discussions With Lanius}}</ref>}}
The '''Believers''' were a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] that practiced the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]], and was initially dismissed as a rumor by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. The cult became a very dangerous threat to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who saw many of their number joining the Believers during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Eventually it was discovered that the cultists were using the ancient [[Almas Sith fortress]] as their base of operations. They had also spread to the ruins of the [[Almas Academy]] after its destruction. All Believers could be recognized by a distinctive [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoo]], which resembled the spire of the Sith fortress on [[Almas]] with a broken [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] at its base.

==History==
===Emergence===
The Believers were a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith-worshipping cult]]<ref name="Belly of the Beaast">{{LivingForce|Metatheran Caution: Belly of the Beaast}}</ref> that formed in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The cult was founded by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Len Markus]],<ref name="The Heart" /> a student of the art of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]],<ref name="The Eye">{{LivingForce|EndTimes: The Eye}}</ref> and they sought to study the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]<ref name="Further Discussions With Lanius" /> and to challenge the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], in order to bring about a new age of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]],<ref name="Caaried Away" /> an ancient order of dark side practitioners.<ref name="A Mon Alone">{{LivingForce|A Mon Alone}}</ref> The cult developed a loosely-formed, matrix structure, with no centralized hierarchy,<ref name="A Dark Cult">{{Wizards|url=rpga/livingforce/article.asp?x=lf/swlf20020411acult&c=swlfc|text=A Dark Cult}}</ref> and one band of Believers fell under the command of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Gar Granga]].<ref name="A Dark Fortress" /> The [[Quermian/Legends|Quermian]]<ref name="Jedi Apprentice">''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: The Followers]]''</ref> Sith [[Historian/Legends|scholar]] [[Murk Lundi]] aided the cult's development<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" /> and the Believers spread across the Cularin system, establishing a presence of the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Almas]] and [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]], and in the cities [[Depatar/Legends|Depatar]], [[Elso Magua]], [[Hylios]], [[Tolea Biqua]] and [[Varna Biqua]] on the planet [[Genarius]].<ref name="A Dark Cult" /> The cult rose into prominence shortly after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]],<ref name="The Clone Wars Campaign Guide">''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]''</ref> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and rumors of the Believers' emergence eventually reached the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Order, but the Jedi were unable to confirm whether the cult existed.<ref name="The Clone Wars Campaign Guide" />

[[File:Believer_Initiate.png|thumb|left|250px|Believers]]

In about [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]],<ref name="31 BBY">The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' states that the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign is set one year after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], an event that is dated to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. The events of ''[[Almas, Sweet Almas: The Kaluthin Are Always Greener|The Kaluthin Are Always Greener]]'' can therefore be dated to about [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].</ref> the researcher [[Garvin Delquist]] discovered a [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]] inside the [[Almas Sith fortress]], an ancient Sith construct that was built by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Rivan]] on the planet [[Almas]], in the Cularin [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The amulet granted Delquist the ability to use the Force and turned him to the dark side, and he subsequently contacted Gar Granga and made an agreement with the Mon Calamari whereby the Believers would take up residence inside the fortress. Delquist also contacted the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Alina Impeveri]], a figure in the Cularin [[Star system/Legends|system]] criminal underworld, and she agreed to join the Believers, bringing with her a large number of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], who enrolled in the cult and based themselves in the Sith fortress. Delquist became the leader of the combined force of Granga's cultists and Impeveri's pirates,<ref name="A Dark Fortress" /> and under his rule, the cult became more centralized and the activities of the movement became more directed toward Almas.<ref name="A Dark Fortress" /><ref name="A Dark Cult" />

===Killing the kaluthin===
{{Quote|The kaluthin are an integral part of the ecosystem—without them, this planet will revert back to being an uninhabitable rock. Something is killing the kaluthin, and we have not yet been able to determine what it is.|[[Gilloma]], on the death of large swaths of kaluthin|<ref name="The Kaluthin Are Always Greener">{{LivingForce|Almas, Sweet Almas: The Kaluthin Are Always Greener}}</ref>}}
The Jedi operated a [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] facility on Almas known as the [[Almas Academy]] and Delquist plotted to drive the Jedi off of the planet by wiping out the [[kaluthin]], a plant engineered by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Rivan to make Almas habitable. Under orders from Delquist, Granga and the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] Dark Jedi [[Kosa-Yin Hadu]] initiated an operation to poison Almas' [[aquifer]]s, to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the kaluthin. Along with a group of Believer thugs, the Dark Jedi pair established a [[Believers camp|temporary camp]] on an Almas hilltop, from which they raided surrounding kaluthin farms and poisoned their [[Water/Legends|water]] supplies with a biological toxin, including an attack on [[Sotose farm|the farm]] of the [[Gender/Legends|man]] [[Rashen Sotose]]. The Believers carried out their operations over a period of three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] and succeeded in killing large swaths of kaluthin.<ref name="The Kaluthin Are Always Greener" />

The [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] at an Almas [[Forard research station|kaluthin research station]] initiated an investigation into the areas of kaluthin extinction, and Hadu and Grangra feared that the station posed a risk to the Believers' operation, believing that the researchers might be able to identify the toxins that the Believers were using to poison the aquifers. In an attempt to mitigate the threat posed by the scientists, Hadu, Granga, and several other Believers traveled to Cularin, where they schemed to stop a shipment of supplies from being transported to the research station, to prevent the scientists from performing their studies. The supplies were scheduled to be transported to Almas by the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Welrin Maduk]] aboard the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Fool's Paradise]]'', so the two Dark Jedi headed to the ''Fool's Paradise''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|landing bay]] in the city of [[Hedrett]] with some other Believers, planning to hijack the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]].The Believer party then approached the ''Fool's Paradise'' while accompanied by a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], and prepared to attack Maduk. However, the [[Heroes of Cularin]], a group of freelance agents who operated in the Cularin system, saw that Maduk was being threatened, and they came to his rescue. As the agents approached the ''Fool's Paradise'', Hadu and Granga fled through a concealed entrance to the hangar, leaving the battle droids and the other Believers to face the agents alone. The agents eventually defeated the remaining Believers, allowing Maduk to travel to Almas unharmed.<ref name="The Kaluthin Are Always Greener" />

Hadu returned to Almas and the Heroes of Cularin started to investigate the death of the kaluthin. Shortly afterward, several Believers traveled in some [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] to a rocky valley on Almas, near to a kaluthin farm, where they began emptying barrels of the biological toxin into a natural well. However, the Believers were sighted by the Heroes of Cularin, who confronted the cultists and obtained from them a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] that revealed the location of the Believers' encampment. The agents then launched an assault of the camp and engaged Hadu and three of his bodyguards in combat, while the other Believers present in the camp powered up their airspeeders and fled. A group Jedi soon arrived on the scene in support of the Heroes of the Cularin, and Hadu and his guards were defeated.<ref name="The Kaluthin Are Always Greener" />

===The kidnap of Misha Quor-Din===
{{Quote|Surrender now, or it will not go well for you.|Jedi Master Lanius Qel-Bertuk, ordering the Believers in the Almas Sith fortress to surrender|<ref name="A Dark Fortress" />}}
During the week prior the Almas' Jedi's [[Ritual Tale of Kibh Jeen|annual recital]] of the story of the Dark Jedi [[Kibh Jeen]], the Believers staged a drive-by attack on [[Remembrance Hall]], a [[Museum/Legends|museum]] located in [[Forard]], Almas' [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]]. While the museum was packed with tourists, three [[Human/Legends|Human]] Believers approached the building in an [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|XP-34 landspeeder]] and hurled a couple of [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] into the hall, which destroyed a portion of the museuems wall and vaporized a [[holoplacard]] created by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Artist/Legends|artist]] [[Zyk-yadoon]]. During the subsequent chaos, a [[Tarasin]] Believer crawled across the damaged wall and painted the words "Death to the Unfaithful" onto it. The Believers then quickly fled the scene of their crime.<ref name="Desert Cries">{{LivingForce|Almas, Sweet Almas: Desert Cries}}</ref> In the aftermath of the attack, Delquist captured the Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Misha Quor-Din]] in the [[Almas Wasteland]]<ref name="Desert Cries" /><ref name="A Dark Fortress" /> and took her to the Sith fortress. Delquist had romantic feelings for Quor-Din but soon after kidnapping her, he realized that the Jedi would never reciprocate his love and the Believers planned to sacrifice her.<ref name="A Dark Fortress" />

Around this time, a report on the activities of the Believers written by the individual [[IP17]] in a format used by the Almas Academy was briefly posted on every [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] news board in the Cularin system. The report noted that indications of significant use of the dark side of the Force among the cult were overstated and also claimed that the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Nirama]] was aware of the Believers' activities and that there was a possibility that the Believers were co-operating with the [[Wyrd]],<ref name="A Dark Cult" /> a group of Tarasin Force [[Witch/Legends|witches]] from the planet Cularin.<ref name="A Plague of Darkness">{{LivingForce|Heart of Evil: A Plague of Darkness}}</ref> The report also stated that the Believers posed a significant threat to the well-being of the system's inhabitants. A number of rumors circulated among the residents of Cularin regarding the origins of the report and some theorized that the report had been written by the Believers themselves, to spread fear.<ref name="A Dark Cult" />

Following Quor-Din's capture, the Believers sent out a number of security details from the Sith fortress, to gather supplies for the cult. A group of Believer pirates boarded a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]-operated freighter and murdered the crew, then stole three [[Submarine/Legends|submersibles]] that the vessel was carrying. The pirates took the submersibles to an underground river on Almas and began to use the vehicles to transport cargo to the Sith fortress, via an [[Water/Legends|underwater]] passage. The Jedi sent out a number of search parties to look for Quor-Din and the Heroes of Cularin formed one such team, and the freelance agents investigated the underwater river. The agents engaged a group of Believer pirates in combat, then traveled aboard one of the submersibles to the Sith fortress. On arrival there, the Believers unloading the cargo from the submersibles mistook the agents for members of the cult and they ordered the agents to unload the craft.<ref name="A Dark Fortress" />

The agents progressed through the fortress and observed Gar Granga leading a group of about twenty cultists in chants of devotion to the dark side, inside the fortress' [[Grand Hall (Almas Sith fortress)|Grand Hall]]. The agents then [[Battle of the Almas Sith fortress|confronted]] and defeated Delquist, Granga and the Believers [[D'randq]] and [[Sellot Kell]], and a klaxon went of, calling for the cultists to withdraw from the fortress. In a state of panic, the Believers attempted to flee the facility and a number attempted to escape aboard a [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]]. The freelance agents liberated Quor-Din and tried to depart the fortress aboard another skiff, but they collided with the Believers skiff and became engaged in a firefight with a large group of the cultists. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lanius Qel-Bertuk]], the headmaster of the Almas Academy, and a number of Jedi soon arrived at the fortress in command of a [[Brigade/Legends|brigade]] of armed [[Troop/Legends|troops]] and stormed the facility, while the remaining Believers scattered in disarray.<ref name="A Dark Fortress" /> Many of the surviving Believers went into hiding<ref name="The Believers">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/lf20050915believers|text=The Believers}}</ref> and a number of the cultists were captured by the Jedi.<ref name="Further Discussions With Lanius" /> To prevent the Believers from returning to the Sith fortress, a Jedi delegation was assigned to keep the facility under permanent guard.<ref name="A Mon Alone" />

===The Pulas===
{{Quote|We have every reason to expect that the Believers—that is the name that has been claimed by the cult of dark side followers that has emerged in Cularin—are trying to stop the crystal from leaving the system|Jedi Master Lanius Qel-Bertuk, on the Pulas|<ref name="Coruscani Dawn" />}}
A team of Jedi researchers recovered a [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] known as the [[Pulas]] from the Sith fortress and they attempted to transport the artifact to the Almas Academy, for safe-keeping. However, the Believers learned of the Jedi's discovery and planned to steal the Pulas, and they attacked the researchers while they were en-route to the Academy. However, the Believers failed to obtain the artifact and the following day, a group of the cultists tried to break into the Academy, in a further attempt to acquire the artifact. The Believers were again unsuccessful and the group committed suicide to prevent themselves from being captured by the Jedi. Realizing that the presence of the Believers on Almas made the Academy an unsuitable location to store the Pulas, Jedi Master Qel-Bertuk assembled a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of starships to fly the artifact to the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where it could be kept in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], the headquarters of the Jedi Order. To made it difficult for the Believers to snatch the Pulas while it was being taken to Coruscant, Qel-Bertuk placed the artifact inside a box of [[Beskar/Legends|Mandalorian iron]] and loaded the box onto one of the ships in his fleet, then placed decoy boxes onto each of the other ships.<ref name="Coruscani Dawn" />

The fleet moved to depart the Cularin system and a Believer-operated [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] attacked one the ships in the [[Cularin comet cloud]], under orders from the cult to destroy the vessel but leave enough of it intact for the Believers to search the wreckage for the Pulas. The attack failed and the Jedi fleet left the system and traveled to Coruscant. However, the Tarasin [[Es'Loma]], an agent of the Wyrd, was serving aboard a the ship carrying one of the Mandalorian iron boxes and she incorrectly identified her vessel's cargo as the Pulas itself, when the ship was in fact instead carrying a fragment of the dark-side-imbued floating city ''[[Conkesta]]''. Es'Loma contacted a group of Believers that were based in a [[Coruscant Believers base|bunker]] in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant Undercity]] and she instigated a plan whereby the Believers would raid her ship and recover the Mandalorian iron box. After Es'Loma's ship had docked with a Coruscant landing platform, a team of black-clad Human Believers led by the [[Omwati/Legends|Omwati]] [[Bleth Fahr]] assailed the crew of Es'Loma's ship. The [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Nysis Belzara]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who had been sent to escort the crew of the ship to safe-house, arrived at the scene and Fahr and his accomplices slew the Jedi, then seized the box containing the fragment of ''Conkesta''.<ref name="Coruscani Dawn" />

The Believers fled in a starship and aimed to return to their base in the Undercity. However, they were chased by the Heroes of Cularin, who had traveled to Coruscant with Qel-Bertuk's fleet, and while attempting to shake off their pursuers, the cultists crashed their ship in the Undercity. They abandoned the Believer [[Blaise]], who had been injured in the accident, then returned to the bunker with the box. A group of Believers gathered in the base and prepared to open the box, and while the cultists were distracted by their prize, the Heroes of Cularin entered the bunker and attacked them. The agents then overpowered the Believers and appropriated the Mandalorian iron box, and the agents transported the fragment of ''Conkesta'' to the Jedi Temple.<ref name="Coruscani Dawn" />

Shortly after the completion the Pulas' transportation, Qel-Bertuk and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] Jedi Master [[Kirlocca]] were interviewed by a reporter on the subject of the Believers' emergence, and they discussed the threat that the cult posed to the residents of the Cularin system.<ref name="Further Discussions With Lanius" />

===Infecting the Caarites===
{{Quote|We did this because it needed to be done. Or, we tried. Everyone suffers. This planet should be no different.|A Believer, on the cult's attempt to disperse the anti-Caarite virus on Caarimon|<ref name="Belly of the Beaast" />}}
Some time later, Len Markus,<ref name="Philology">{{LivingForce|Below the Belt: Philology}}</ref> the founder of the Believers,<ref name="The Heart" /> seized an ancient [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] known as the [[Darkstaff]] from the [[Cularin asteroid belt]], causing the Cularin system to be transported ten years into the future<ref name="Philology" /> to about 21 BBY,<ref name="21 BBY">The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' states that the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign is set one year after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], an event that is dated to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Below the Belt: Philology|Philology]]'' [[Cularin's disappearance|moved]] the campaign forward ten years to the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the events of ''[[Metatheran Caution: Caaried Away|Caaried Away]]'' can therefore be dated to about [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].</ref> during the era of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Philology" /> The artifact possessed Markus and under the Darkstaff's influence, he rose through the ranks of the Believers to become the cult's new leader.<ref name="The Clone Wars Campaign Guide" /> The Believers developed into a formidable force<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" /> and during the Clone Wars a number of Jedi stationed in the Cularin system turned to the dark side and became members of the cult.<ref name="The Clone Wars Campaign Guide" /> The Believers established themselves as a power in the criminal underworld of the Cularin system<ref name="Caaried Away" /> and in about [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],<ref name="21 BBY" /> they initiated a secret campaign to rid themselves of any criminal competitors and selected the [[Metatheran Cartel]] as their first target.<ref name="Caaried Away" /> The Cartel was a trading concern that was led by members of the [[Caarite]] and [[Filordus|Filordi]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="Living Force Campaign Guide">''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]''</ref> and to counter the concern's influence, the Believers procured a [[Anti-Caarite virus|deadly virus]] that was fatal to Caarites. The cultists further refined the virus and they selected a [[Metatheran Cartel Tilnes base|hidden Metatheran Cartel base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Tilnes]] for use as a testing ground for the infection, because the Cartel would not publicize an outbreak inside the facility, due to the secrecy surrounding the base's existence. They released the virus into the facility via a bomb that they had wired into the complex's circuitry, and the infection wiped out all of the Caarites inside the base, with the execeptions of the [[Overseer of Cartel Operations, Tilnes Special Detachment|overseer]] [[Ko Bas]] and two others, who became mutated and were transformed into raging killers.<ref name="Caaried Away" />

A team of six Human Believers gathered in a [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] in Tolea Biqua on Genarius and from there they traveled to the Cartel base on Tilnes, to survey the results of the outbreak. However, while they were inside the facility, the base was hit by a burst of [[Electromagnetic pulse/Legends|electromagnetic pulse]] produced by Tilnes' core, which shut down the base's power systems. When the power came back on after the pulse had passed, the base's emergency seal activated and trapped the Believer team inside the facility. The mutated Ko Bas captured and killed four of the Believers, and the two survivors, [[Aris/Legends|Aris]] and [[Osia]], took refuge inside the complex's security office.<ref name="Caaried Away" />

The Believers carried out improvements to and further production of the virus from a [[S-Ipsus Believers laboratory|secret laboratory]] located inside the manufacturing station [[S-Ipsus]], on Genarius. Once the virus was ready for deployment, the Believers sent a transmission to the laboratory ordering the facility's inhabitants to abandon S-Ipsus and destroy any evidence of the cult's presence there. However, unknown to the cultists, the transmission was intercepted by both the crime lord Nirama and the Cartel. Convinced that the Believers were a threat to the Cularin system, Nirama hired the Heroes of Cularin to destroy the virus production facility, while the Cartel independently sent a strike team of their own to destroy the facility.<ref name="Open Aarms" /> However, before the agents and the Cartel arrived at the base, the Believers shipped large quantities of the infection from the laboratory inside three transports, which then headed to the [[Caarimoos system]] via circuitous routes, in preparation for a dispersal of the virus on [[Caarimon]], the Caarite [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="Belly of the Beaast" /> Shortly afterward, the Heroes of Cularin and the Cartel team landed on S-Ipsus and the agents confronted the Believer [[Canduri]] on a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] production line, while the Cartel team launched an attack on the laboratory. The Believers inside the base were taken by surprise and the Cartel wiped out all of the laboratory's inhabitants.<ref name="Open Aarms" />

The Believers hired the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Melo Centris]] to help them to disperse the virus in Caarimon's upper atmosphere, which they hoped would kill more than half of Caarimon's native Caarite population. In addition, the cultists schemed to cause a major environmental catastrophe on Caarimon, by crashing [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] packed with explosives into some fault-lines in the [[Desolation]] region of Caarimon, to trigger a [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquake]]. The three Believer transports from Genarius arrived in the Carimoos system under the escort of the [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] ''[[Ku'Amo]]'', and two of the starships docked with [[Creautaa-V]], a research station that orbited Caarimon. Centris and a group of Believers led by the former Jedi [[Crymsin Ost]], the commander of the Caarimon operation, entered the station and released the virus, killing all of the Caarites aboard the facility. Meanwhile, the third transport landed in the city [[Joventusek]] on Caarimon and a team of four Human Believers disembarked from the vessel. The Believer team then hired some [[T3-OR]] [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] and traveled to the city [[Efellis]], where they contacted a local [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] and obtained some data regarding the world's fault-lines. Afterward, the team biked to the Desolation and planted a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]], to guide the explosive-filled escape pods to the fault-lines. To prevent anyone from interfering in the cult's plans, the Believer team took up a position 30 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] north of Joventusek, under orders to kill any off-worlders that attempted to travel toward the Desolation.<ref name="Belly of the Beaast" />

The [[Cularin Militia]], a military force that was active in the Cularin system, received intelligence reports that stated that a number of Believers were converging on Caarimon, and the Militia deduced that the cultists planned to release the anti-Caarite virus on the planet. To prevent the Believers from completing their plan, the Cularin Militia hired the Heroes of Cularin to thwart the cult's activities on Caarimon. The agents traveled to Caarimon and attempted to make their way to the Desolation. However, en-route the agents were ambushed by the Believer guards stationed north of Joventusek, who engaged the agents in a speeder bike chase. However, the agents defeated the Believer team and disabled the Believers' homing beacon. The agents proceeded to fly to Creautaa-V and they confronted Centris and Crymsin Ost's Believers aboard the station and prevented them from releasing the virus. During the skirmish, the agents also recovered information from Centris' [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] that implicated the [[Thaereian military]], an armed force based in the [[Thaereian system|Thaere system]], as having conspired with the Believers to attempt to wipe out the Caarites. However, the agents were uncertain as to whether the data was genuine.<ref name="Belly of the Beaast" />

To congratulate the Heroes of Cularin for preventing the Believers from releasing the anti-Caarite virus on Caarimon, the Metatheran Cartel held a celebratory party on [[Dorumaa]], the moon of Almas. During the event, a rumor circulated that someone was planning to attack the party, and some of the attendees falsely suspected that the Believers were going to launch a strike against the celebration.<ref name="Paarty On!">{{LivingForce|Paarty On!}}</ref>

===Later operations===
At some point afterward, the Heroes of Cularin came into contact with the "[[Oblee|shadow lurkers]]," disembodied spirits of the Oblee species that resided in the Cularin [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]]. The Darkstaff was able to draw energy from the shadow lurkers via three [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] shards that were located in the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] belt and the spirits requested that the freelance agents destroy the shards, to sever the Oblees' connection to the staff. The Darkstaff sensed that someone other than the shadow lurkers had came into contact with the shards and at the artifact's behest, Len Markus sent a group of Believers to the asteroid belt, under orders to either kill the individuals that had disturbed the shards, or alternately to recover the three shards. The group of Believers, which included the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]] [[Gaar]] and [[Ulis]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Rorvek]], and several others, landed on the asteroid that housed the shards, and they attacked the Heroes of Cularin. However, the agents defeated the Believers<ref name="Recursion" /> and freed the Oblee from the clutches of the Darkstaff.<ref name="The Oblee">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20050106|text=The Oblee}}</ref>

At around this time, a [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] posted a message on the [[Cularin holonet node]], which discussed the creation and role of the [[Sith battlelord]]s, a class of [[warrior]]s that had served Darth Rivan. However, the Cularin system's media subsequently refused to discuss the article, out of fear that doing so would attract the attention of the Believers to the posting.<ref name="Sith Battlelords">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/lf20050901sithbattlelords|text=Sith Battlelords}}</ref>

The Believers began working with the Wyrd and the cultists hoped to be able to usurp the Tarasin witches' Force abilities, to use the Wyrd's powers against the Jedi.<ref name="Decision: Almas">{{LivingForce|Decision: Almas}}</ref> The two organizations planned to launch a strike against the Almas Jedi<ref name="Decision: Coruscant">{{LivingForce|Decision: Coruscant}}</ref> and they also plotted to fracture the [[Cularin Question|Cularin self-governance]] movement, a political campaign backed by the Cularin Militia that sought to force the Thaereian military to withdraw its troops from the Cularin system. To that end, the Believers and the Wyrd conspired to attempt to link the self-governance movement with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]],<ref name="Decision: Almas" /> a separatist government that opposed the Galactic Republic.<ref name="The Clone Wars Campaign Guide" /> The Believers allied themselves with the Separatists,<ref name="Destruction" /> and the cultists and the Wyrd agreed to share with the Confederacy documentation regarding how the self-governance movement could be undermined. In addition, the cult also established links with the Thaereian military.<ref name="Decision: Almas" /> However, unknown to the Believers, the cult's collusion with the Separatists, the Thaereians and the Wyrd arose as a result of the machinations of the Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]].<ref name="Decision: Almas" /><ref name="Destruction" /> The Believers eventually occupied an [[abandoned Separatist base]] located in a [[Unidentified star system (abandoned Separatist base)|deserted star system]].<ref name="Destruction" />

The Clone Wars progressed and at some point, the protest group [[Truth For Cularin]] produced a number of HoloNet documentaries, in which they speculated on issues that affected the Cularin system. In one such documentary, the Human [[Jarik Vuintor]] claimed that the Believers were an arm of the Jedi Order, to which the Jedi transferred failed Jedi students that were found to be not suited to the rigors of the life of the Jedi. Vuintor also speculated that the cult was funded by [[Lavina Durada-Vashne Wren|Lavin Wren]], the Cularin system's [[Senator/Legends|senator]], to allow Wren to use the cult to further her political career.<ref name="The Believers" />

In about 19 BBY,<ref name="19 BBY">The [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] adventure ''[[Heart of Evil: A Hero Seeks Not Vengeance|A Hero Seeks Not Vengeance]]'' is set concurrently with the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], an event that is dated to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. ''A Plague of Darkness'' is set shortly before the events of ''A Hero Seeks Not Vengeance'' and can thus be dated to circa 19 BBY.</ref> Len Markus and elements of the Believers provided the Wyrd with information that Markus had obtained from the study of Sith mutational practices. With the help of Markus' information, the Wyrd developed the [[Ritual of the Power of the Dark]], which they attempted to use to [[Plague of Darkness|turn]] the [[Heart Tree|World Tree]], an inter-connected network of [[Ch'hala tree|ch'hala]] trees that grew on Cularin, to the dark side. However, the Wyrd's plans failed and the Cularin Armed Forces subsequently obtained evidence that the Believers had supplied the Wyrd with the knowledge that had enabled them to perform the ritual. The CAF also learned that the Believers were operating from the abandoned Separatist facility and the Armed Forces began planning an attack on the base, to capture Markus.<ref name="A Plague of Darkness" />
